message:
fix(iconv): bound encoding-name reads in iconv_open (bd-z4k96 class)

REVIEW round 3: iconv_open dereferenced `tocode` and `fromcode` via
CStr::from_ptr at lines 127-129 with no length bound. A caller that
passes a non-NUL-terminated encoding-name pointer walked arbitrary
process memory until an accidental zero byte — same info-leak /
segfault class that bd-z4k96 closed for setlocale, textdomain,
bindtextdomain, and newlocale.

Reachability: every gettext-enabled or i18n-aware program that opens
a converter with caller-supplied encoding names is exposed. The
hardening matches the locale defense exactly: introduce a local
read_bounded_cstr helper that wraps scan_c_string + known_remaining
and treat unbounded inputs as EINVAL.

Full abi suite: 2976/2976 pass (41/41 iconv tests including the
existing concurrency regression).

DB write path remains wedged behind two stuck `br sync` processes;
filing this defect through the commit message in lieu of a bead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
